title: Annapurna Chalisa
intro: Shri Annapurna Chalisa - Hymn to Goddess Annapurna
description:
॥ Sri Annapurna Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Vishweshwar padpadam ki raj nij sheesh lagaay,
Annapoorne, tav suyash baranau kavi matilay.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Nitya anand karini maata,
Var aru abhay bhav prakhyata.
Jai! Saundarya sindhu jag janani,
Akhil paap har bhav-bhay harani.
Shvet badan par shvet basan puni,
Santan tuv pad sevat rishi-muni.
Kaashi puraadheeshwari maata,
Maheshwari sakal jag traata.
Vrishabharudh naam Rudraani,
Vishwa vihaarini jai! Kalyaani.
Patidevata suteet shiromani,
Padavi praapt keenh Giri Nandini.
Pati vichhoh dukh sahi nahin paava,
Yog agni tab badan jaraava.
Deh tajat Shiv charan sanehu,
Raakhehu jaat Himagiri geheu.
Prakati Girija naam dharayo,
Ati anand bhavan manh chhaayo.
Narad ne tab tohin bharamayahu,
Byah karan hit path padhayahu.
Brahma Varun Kuber ganaye,
Devraj aadik kahi gaye.
Sab devan ko sujas bakhani,
Mati palatan ki man manh thaani.
Achal raheen tum pran par dhanya,
Keehni siddh Himachal kanya.
Nij kau tab Narad ghabraye,
Tab pran pooran mantra padhaye.
Karan hetu tap tohin upadesheu,
Sant bachan tum satya parekheu.
Gagan gira suni tari na taare,
Brahma tab tuv paas padhare.
Kaheu putri var maangu anoopa,
Dehun aaj tuv mati anuroopa.
Tum tap keenh alaukik bhaari,
Kasht uthayahu ati sukumari.
Ab sandeh chhaandi kachhu moson,
Hai saugandh nahin chhal toson.
Karat ved vid Brahma jaanahu,
Vachan mor yah sancha maanahu.
Taji sankoch kahahu nij ichha,
Dehaun main manmaani bhiksha.
Suni Brahma ki madhuri baani,
Mukh son kachhu musukaya Bhawani.
Boli tum ka kahahu Vidhata,
Tum to jagke srashtadhata.
Mam kaamna gupt nahin tonson,
Kahwava chaahahu ka monson.
Daksha yagya mahan marti baara,
Shambhunaath puni hohi hamara.
So ab milahi mohin manbhaye,
Kahi tathastu vidhi dhaam sidhaye.
Tab Girija Shankar tav bhayau,
Phal kaamna sanshayo gayau.
Chandra koti ravi koti prakasha,
Tab aanan mahan karat nivaasa.
Mala pustak ankush sohai,
Kar mahan apar paash man mohai.
Annapoorne! Sadapoorne,
Aj anavagh anant poorne.
Kripa saagari kshemankari maa,
Bhav vibhuti aanand bhari maa.
Kamal vilochana vilasit bhale,
Devi Kalike Chandi karale.
Tum Kailash maanh hai Girija,
Vilasi aanand saath Sindhuja.
Swarg Mahalakshmi kahalayi,
Martya lok Lakshmi padpaayi.
Vilasi sab mahan sarva sarupa,
Sevat tohin amar pur bhupa.
Jo padhihahi yah tav chalisa,
Phal paihahi shubh saakhi Eesa.
Praat samay jo jan man layo,
Padhihahi bhakti suruchi aghikayo.
Stree kalatra pati mitra putra yut,
Parmeshwarya laabh lahi adbhut.
Raaj vimukh ko raaj divavai,
Jas tero jan sujas badhavai.
Paath maha mud mangal daata,
Bhakt manovanchhit nidhi paata.

॥ Doha ॥
Jo yah chalisa subhag,
Padhi naavainge maath,
Tinke kaaraj siddh sab,
Saakhi Kashi Naath.
meaning:
॥ Sri Annapurna Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Vishweshwar padpadam ki raj nij sheesh lagaay,
[I apply the dust of Lord Shiva's lotus feet to my head.]
Annapoorne, tav suyash baranau kavi matilay.
[O Annapurna, I narrate your glorious deeds with my humble poetic ability.]

॥ Chaupai ॥
Nitya anand karini maata,
[Mother, who provides eternal joy.]
Var aru abhay bhav prakhyata.
[Known for granting boons and fearlessness.]

Jai! Saundarya sindhu jag janani,
[Victory to you, the ocean of beauty and mother of the world.]
Akhil paap har bhav-bhay harani.
[You destroy all sins and remove the fear of the world.]

Shvet badan par shvet basan puni,
[With a white radiant form and white garments.]
Santan tuv pad sevat rishi-muni.
[Sages and saints serve at your feet.]

Kaashi puraadheeshwari maata,
[Mother, the Queen of the city of Kashi.]
Maheshwari sakal jag traata.
[Maheshwari, you are the savior of the entire world.]

Vrishabharudh naam Rudraani,
[You ride the bull and are known as Rudrani.]
Vishwa vihaarini jai! Kalyaani.
[Victory to you, the auspicious one who dwells in the universe.]

Patidevata suteet shiromani,
[Crest jewel of devotion to your husband.]
Padavi praapt keenh Giri Nandini.
[Daughter of the mountain, you attained this high position.]

Pati vichhoh dukh sahi nahin paava,
[Unable to bear the pain of separation from your husband.]
Yog agni tab badan jaraava.
[You burned your body in the fire of yoga.]

Deh tajat Shiv charan sanehu,
[Even while leaving the body, you loved Shiva’s feet.]
Raakhehu jaat Himagiri geheu.
[And he took you to the home of the Himalayas.]

Prakati Girija naam dharayo,
[You reappeared and were named Girija.]
Ati anand bhavan manh chhaayo.
[Great joy filled the abode.]

Narad ne tab tohin bharamayahu,
[Narada then caused you to be perplexed.]
Byah karan hit path padhayahu.
[And taught you the path for the sake of marriage.]

Brahma Varun Kuber ganaye,
[He mentioned Brahma, Varuna, and Kubera.]
Devraj aadik kahi gaye.
[And the King of Gods, Indra.]

Sab devan ko sujas bakhani,
[By recounting the praises of all these gods.]
Mati palatan ki man manh thaani.
[He intended to change your mind.]

Achal raheen tum pran par dhanya,
[You remained firm on your vow, O blessed one.]
Keehni siddh Himachal kanya.
[You, the daughter of Himachal, attained your goal.]

Nij kau tab Narad ghabraye,
[Narada became nervous seeing this.]
Tab pran pooran mantra padhaye.
[And taught you the mantra to fulfill your vow.]

Karan hetu tap tohin upadesheu,
[He instructed you to perform penance.]
Sant bachan tum satya parekheu.
[You tested and found the words of the saint to be true.]

Gagan gira suni tari na taare,
[Hearing the voice from the sky, you did not waver.]
Brahma tab tuv paas padhare.
[Brahma then arrived before you.]

Kaheu putri var maangu anoopa,
[He said, "Daughter, ask for a unique boon.]
Dehun aaj tuv mati anuroopa.
[I will give you today according to your desire."]

Tum tap keenh alaukik bhaari,
[You have performed immense divine penance.]
Kasht uthayahu ati sukumari.
[You endured hardships, O tender one.]

Ab sandeh chhaandi kachhu moson,
[Now leave your doubts and ask something of me.]
Hai saugandh nahin chhal toson.
[I swear there is no deception with you.]

Karat ved vid Brahma jaanahu,
[Know Brahma as the knower of the Vedas.]
Vachan mor yah sancha maanahu.
[Consider these words of mine to be true.]

Taji sankoch kahahu nij ichha,
[Cast aside hesitation and express your desire.]
Dehaun main manmaani bhiksha.
[I will grant you the alms of your heart’s choice.]

Suni Brahma ki madhuri baani,
[Hearing Brahma’s sweet words.]
Mukh son kachhu musukaya Bhawani.
[Goddess Bhawani smiled a little.]

Boli tum ka kahahu Vidhata,
[You said, "What are you saying, O Creator?]
Tum to jagke srashtadhata.
[You are the architect of the world.]

Mam kaamna gupt nahin tonson,
[My desire is not hidden from you.]
Kahwava chaahahu ka monson.
[What do you wish to make me say?"]

Daksha yagya mahan marti baara,
[Since I died in Daksha’s sacrifice.]
Shambhunaath puni hohi hamara.
[May Shambhunath (Shiva) be mine again.]

So ab milahi mohin manbhaye,
[May he meet me as my heart desires.]
Kahi tathastu vidhi dhaam sidhaye.
[Saying 'So be it', the Creator departed.]

Tab Girija Shankar tav bhayau,
[Then Girija became Shankar’s consort.]
Phal kaamna sanshayo gayau.
[The fruit of desire was attained, and doubts vanished.]

Chandra koti ravi koti prakasha,
[Millions of moons and suns reside in your light.]
Tab aanan mahan karat nivaasa.
[They reside in the light of your face.]

Mala pustak ankush sohai,
[The garland, book, and goad look beautiful.]
Kar mahan apar paash man mohai.
[In your hand, the rope captivates the mind.]

Annapoorne! Sadapoorne,
[O Annapurna! Always complete.]
Aj anavagh anant poorne.
[Unborn, sinless, and infinitely full.]

Kripa saagari kshemankari maa,
[O ocean of mercy, Mother who brings welfare.]
Bhav vibhuti aanand bhari maa.
[Mother filled with the opulence of the world and joy.]

Kamal vilochana vilasit bhale,
[Lotus-eyed one with a radiant forehead.]
Devi Kalike Chandi karale.
[O Mother Kalika, fierce Chandi.]

Tum Kailash maanh hai Girija,
[You are Girija on Kailash.]
Vilasi aanand saath Sindhuja.
[Residing in joy with the daughter of the ocean (Lakshmi).]

Swarg Mahalakshmi kahalayi,
[You are called Mahalakshmi in heaven.]
Martya lok Lakshmi padpaayi.
[And you attained the status of Lakshmi in the mortal world.]

Vilasi sab mahan sarva sarupa,
[You reside in all, in every form.]
Sevat tohin amar pur bhupa.
[The kings of the immortal realm serve you.]

Jo padhihahi yah tav chalisa,
[Whoever reads this Chalisa of yours.]
Phal paihahi shubh saakhi Eesa.
[They will receive the fruits, with Lord Shiva as the witness.]

Praat samay jo jan man layo,
[Those who read this in the morning with a focused mind.]
Padhihahi bhakti suruchi aghikayo.
[With devotion and deep interest.]

Stree kalatra pati mitra putra yut,
[Along with spouse, friends, and children.]
Parmeshwarya laabh lahi adbhut.
[They will attain wonderful, supreme prosperity.]

Raaj vimukh ko raaj divavai,
[You bestow kingdoms upon those who have lost them.]
Jas tero jan sujas badhavai.
[Your devotee increases their own fame by your glory.]

Paath maha mud mangal daata,
[The reading of this is a great giver of happiness.]
Bhakt manovanchhit nidhi paata.
[The devotee obtains the treasure they desire.]

॥ Doha ॥
Jo yah chalisa subhag, padhi naavainge maath,
[Whoever reads this beautiful Chalisa and bows their head.]
Tinke kaaraj siddh sab, saakhi Kashi Naath.
[All their tasks will be accomplished, with Lord Kashi Nath as witness.]

faq.q1: Why should the Annapurna Chalisa be recited?
faq.a1: Maa Annapurna is the Goddess of food and abundance. Reciting this Chalisa is a prayer to ensure that there is never a shortage of food or resources in our home.
faq.q2: Should it be read in the kitchen?
faq.a2: Yes, the kitchen is considered the abode of Maa Annapurna. Reciting it in a clean kitchen is very auspicious as it shows respect for the food we eat.
faq.q3: What is the main benefit of the Annapurna Chalisa?
faq.a3: Regular recitation brings prosperity and abundance to the house, removes poverty, and ensures that food is never wasted.
faq.q4: What is the best day to recite it?
faq.a4: You can recite it daily, but Tuesday and Friday are considered excellent days to seek the special blessings of Maa Annapurna.
faq.q5: Should a special offering (Bhog) be made while reading it?
faq.a5: Maa Annapurna appreciates simplicity. You can offer fruits, mishri (sugar candy), or kheer, but the most important offering is your feeling of gratitude for the food.
